# Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview
This repository is for designing flag football plays for a 7-on-7 flag football playbook.

## Player Roster & Roles
| Label | Role | Color |
|-------|------|-------|
| Q | Quarterback | Black |
| C | Center | Yellow |
| X | #1 Receiver | Red |
| Z | #2 Receiver | Blue |
| Y | Slot Receiver | Green |
| H | Hybrid Receiver/Blocker | Orange |
| R | Running Back/Blocker | Purple |

## Formation Defaults
- **QB (Q)** lines up 7 yards behind the line of scrimmage (under center or shotgun)
- **RB (R)** lines up even with the QB, offset to the left side

## Rules & Constraints
- This is **7-on-7 flag football** (no offensive line, no tackling)
- Defense may rush from the **1-yard line**, so every play must have a **quick/hot option** for the QB
- On deeper developing routes, the **R should be used as a pass protector/blocker** to give the QB time
- The **H can also be motioned into a blocking position** to provide protection on longer-developing plays

## File Structure
- Plays are **grouped by concept/category** (e.g., quick game, intermediate, deep shots, motion plays, run plays)
- Each category lives in its own markdown file
- A top-level `PLAYBOOK.md` serves as the index

## Play Format
- Plays are written in **Markdown** with an **SVG diagram** (`plays/svg/<name>.svg`) for the field visual
- SVG diagrams are generated by running `python3 generate_plays.py` from the repo root
- Use **standard football route and play names** (e.g., Mesh, Dagger, Slant, Curl, Flat, Wheel, Post, Corner, Go, etc.)
- Each play entry should include:
  1. **Play name** (standard football terminology)
  2. **SVG diagram** embedded with `![Play Name](svg/play-name.svg)`
  3. **Route assignments table** — one row per player (include depth in yards)
  4. **QB reads** — 🔥 Hot read, 🎯 Primary read, ↩️ Secondary read
  5. **Notes** — motion keys, blocking assignments, coverage reads, situational usage

## SVG Diagram Conventions
- Player dots are color-coded by position (see roster table above)
- **Route arrows are drawn in the player's position color** (X=red, Z=blue, Y=green, H=orange, R=purple)
- **Hot read route** = same player color but **dashed stroke**
- **Gray curved arc** = blocking zone (upfield of the blocker)
- **Orange dashed L-path** = pre-snap motion (player dot stays at origin; open dashed circle marks the destination)
- **Green dashed arrow** = backward lateral pass
- Dashed vertical line between C and Q = snap
- Yard lines shown at 5, 10, 15 yards above LOS
- Route depth and name are documented in the route table below each diagram

## Motion Rules
- Pre-snap motion **must go backward (away from LOS) first**, then travel **parallel to the LOS** — never toward the LOS
- The motion path is drawn as an **L-shape**: step back, then slide laterally
- The **player dot remains at the original position**; the post-motion landing spot is shown as an **open dashed circle** with the player label
- Post-snap routes start from the landing spot and are drawn as solid arrows in the player's color

## Play Design Guidelines
- Every play must have a **hot/quick option** (3-5 yards, beats the blitz)
- Indicate when **R or H is blocking** vs. running a route
- On deeper plays, assign **R and/or H as blockers**
- Use **H motion** when it adds value (e.g., motion to block, motion to create a pick/rub concept)
- On bubble screens, show **H driving upfield** post-snap as the lead block and **Y catching behind H** then running through the created lane
